title: Verify Divider Placement In The Z-Axis Cable Carrier
summary: Inspect the Z-Axis cable carrier to verify that dividers are present where applicable and positioned correctly between the cable and the air line so the air line does not contact the FFC. The source states dividers are included in every second link, several missing dividers are acceptable, and installed dividers should be fully to one side against the air line.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when inspecting the Z-Axis cable carrier condition or verifying whether divider placement matches the documented configuration in section 7.3.16.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to determine an exact required count of missing dividers beyond the source statement that several missing dividers are acceptable.
Do not use this procedure as a replacement or repair procedure for the Z-Axis cable carrier.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the visual inspection of the Z-Axis cable carrier, verify divider placement, and escalate if divider position cannot be verified or if the air line appears able to contact the FFC.

safety_notes: Use only the visual inspection criteria supported by the source.
Do not infer a stricter divider quantity requirement than the source provides.
Escalate if divider position cannot be verified or if the air line appears able to contact the FFC.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the Z-Axis cable carrier

access_required: Visual access to the Z-Axis cable carrier

visual_references.description: Figure 7-17 Cable Carrier on page 169 showing the Z-Axis cable carrier referenced by the inspection criteria.
Figure 3-4 identifying the Z-axis cable carrier location within the tipper assembly.
Figure 7-14 Flat-Flex Cable for reference to the FFC mentioned in the divider positioning criteria.

steps.title: Locate the Z-Axis cable carrier
steps.purpose: Identify the correct assembly before checking divider placement.
steps.instruction: Locate the Z-Axis cable carrier and visually inspect the cable carrier links.
steps.expected_result: The Z-Axis cable carrier is identified and its links are visible for inspection.
steps.healthy_condition: The cable carrier assembly and individual links can be visually identified.
steps.failure_condition: The cable carrier or its links cannot be clearly located or viewed well enough to inspect divider placement.

actions.label: Locate cable carrier
actions.exact_action: Locate the Z-Axis cable carrier and visually inspect the links.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-17 to identify the cable carrier assembly and the links where dividers are installed.
Use Figure 3-4 to identify the Z-axis cable carrier location within the tipper components.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the divider position cannot be verified because the cable carrier cannot be clearly inspected.

steps.title: Identify divider locations in the links
steps.purpose: Verify where dividers are expected within the cable carrier.
steps.instruction: Identify the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link.
steps.expected_result: Divider locations can be recognized in the links where they are intended to be installed.
steps.healthy_condition: Dividers are identifiable between the cable and the air line in every second link where present.
steps.failure_condition: Divider locations cannot be identified or the relationship between the cable, air line, and divider is unclear.

actions.label: Identify dividers
actions.exact_action: Identify the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the divider location between the cable and air line in the cable carrier links.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if divider position cannot be verified.

steps.title: Assess missing dividers against source allowance
steps.purpose: Apply the source guidance on acceptable missing divider condition without over-interpreting it.
steps.instruction: Check whether some dividers are missing and note that the source states it is acceptable for several of these dividers to be missing.
steps.expected_result: The observed condition is compared to the source statement that several missing dividers are acceptable.
steps.healthy_condition: Some missing dividers may be present and are not automatically treated as a fault because the source allows several to be missing.
steps.failure_condition: The inspection result cannot be interpreted without inventing a required divider count or the condition cannot be verified.

actions.label: Check for missing dividers
actions.exact_action: Check whether some dividers are missing and note that the source states it is acceptable for several of these dividers to be missing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the cable carrier figure as a visual reference while checking whether divider positions are populated or missing.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the condition cannot be verified without assuming an exact allowed number of missing dividers.

steps.title: Verify divider position against the air line
steps.purpose: Confirm the installed dividers are positioned as specified by the source.
steps.instruction: Verify the installed dividers are all the way to one side against the air line.
steps.expected_result: Installed dividers are positioned fully to one side against the air line.
steps.healthy_condition: Each installed divider observed is all the way to one side against the air line.
steps.failure_condition: An installed divider is not fully to one side against the air line or divider position cannot be verified.

actions.label: Check divider position
actions.exact_action: Verify the installed dividers are all the way to one side against the air line.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Inspect divider placement relative to the air line and confirm the divider is fully to one side against the air line.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if divider position cannot be verified.
Escalate if any installed divider is not all the way to one side against the air line.

steps.title: Confirm separation between air line and FFC
steps.purpose: Verify the intended outcome of divider placement is achieved.
steps.instruction: Confirm the divider position prevents the air line from contacting the FFC.
steps.expected_result: The air line is kept from contacting the FFC by the divider placement.
steps.healthy_condition: The air line does not contact and does not appear able to contact the FFC based on divider placement.
steps.failure_condition: The air line appears able to contact the FFC or actual contact is observed.

actions.label: Confirm air line separation from FFC
actions.exact_action: Confirm the divider position prevents the air line from contacting the FFC.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Inspect the cable carrier routing to confirm divider placement keeps the air line separated from the FFC.
Use the FFC figure as a reference for the flat-flex cable mentioned in the inspection criterion.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the air line appears able to contact the FFC.
Escalate if divider position cannot be verified.

success_criteria: The Z-Axis cable carrier can be visually inspected.
Divider locations between the cable and air line in every second link are identifiable.
Observed missing dividers are evaluated using only the source statement that several missing dividers are acceptable.
Installed dividers are all the way to one side against the air line.
The air line is prevented from contacting the FFC.
healthy_conditions: The cable carrier links are visible for inspection.
Dividers are identifiable where present between the cable and the air line in every second link.
Several missing dividers may be present and remain acceptable per the source.
Installed dividers are fully to one side against the air line.
The air line does not contact the FFC.
failure_conditions: The cable carrier or divider positions cannot be clearly inspected.
An installed divider is not all the way to one side against the air line.
The air line appears able to contact the FFC.
The inspection would require inventing an exact allowed number of missing dividers not provided by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if divider position cannot be verified.
Escalate if the air line appears able to contact the FFC.
Do not declare failure solely from several missing dividers because the source states that several missing dividers are acceptable.
Escalate for further review if the observed condition cannot be assessed without assuming details not stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary visual reference for the Z-Axis cable carrier and divider inspection.
Reference for locating the Z-axis cable carrier within the tipper assembly.
Reference for the FFC mentioned in the divider positioning criterion.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide an estimated inspection time.
The source packet does not explicitly state whether production stop is required for this inspection.
The source packet does not explicitly state whether LOTO is required for this inspection-only verification.
The source packet does not provide a more specific acceptance threshold for how many dividers may be missing beyond stating that several missing dividers are acceptable.
